opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My legs and backside are covered in hundreds of painful, itchy white pimples. It flared up completely out of nowhere after my long run yesterday.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It came up fast overnight, the itch is so bad I couldn't sleep, and it looks like it's spreading all over the lower half of me — I'm worried it's some kind of infection.

symptom_narrative.onset: about 24 hours ago, the day after a long-distance run in tight leggings in hot, humid weather
symptom_narrative.duration: around a day, and it came up very quickly
symptom_narrative.character: hundreds of tiny white-headed pimples with a little red ring around each one, very itchy and a bit tender; some have popped and left tiny crusts
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the itch is unbearable and stopped me sleeping; it's more itchy than sore
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: both thighs, my calves and my backside — the areas where my tight running leggings were
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant and worst overnight since it came up
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing much; a cool shower settles the itch for a moment
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: scratching, heat and sweat make the itch worse
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: no fever, no chills, I don't feel unwell in myself, no big painful lumps or boils, and no cold-sore-type blisters or mouth sores — it's just the little pimples on my legs and bum
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I've had the odd few of these on my thighs after gym sessions before, but never hundreds all at once like this
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I couldn't sleep and it's uncomfortable to sit

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: no regular medications, and I haven't started anything new

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: I do a lot of long-distance running and wear tight lycra leggings; this came up after a long run in humid weather when I sweated heavily and stayed in the damp gear for a while. No spa, hot tub or pool.

objective_data_offered.value: hundreds of tiny 1-2 mm white-headed spots on both thighs, calves and buttocks; each one looks like it sits right on top of a hair with a small red ring around it, and the skin in between the spots looks completely normal; a few have burst leaving little honey-coloured crusts
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: described by the patient over video; not a clinician examination

objective_data_offered.value: it's only where my tight running leggings and sweat were — both thighs, calves and buttocks, fairly evenly on both sides; nothing on my face, trunk, arms or the front of my body
worn out and fidgety after no sleep, keeps shifting because it's uncomfortable to sit and the itch is constant
I haven't taken my temperature but I don't feel hot, shivery or unwell in myself
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: patient impression only; no thermometer reading taken
